Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Theme post: The AI Execution Gap (wraps ai-execution blog)
Description:
Topic: Theme post — the AI execution gap: AI can write the message, but the win is delivery + proof it landed. Wraps the ai-execution blog (same-day, 7/6).
Post type: static-theme
Wraps: ai-execution-internal-communications
Copy:
Every internal comms platform shipped an AI writer in the last eighteen months, and the demos all look the same. Blank field, a prompt, a finished newsletter in nine seconds.

It's a good trick. It also solves the one part of the job IC teams least needed solved. Writing a clear announcement was never the bottleneck.

The hard part starts after the draft. Who needs this message. Which channel will they see. Did the night shift get it before they clocked in. Did anyone read past the subject line. None of the AI demos show you that.

Think of it as four layers: Generate, Personalize, Deliver, Land. Generation is layer one, and it's table stakes now. The next time a vendor demos an AI that writes a newsletter in nine seconds, ask what it does in the next nine minutes. That answer tells you whether you are buying a writer or an execution layer.

The four layers, and the 5 questions to ask any AI-for-IC vendor, are on the blog: [Furqan inserts live cerkl.com/blog/ai-execution-internal-communications on publish]

Asset spec: Numbered 4-layer stack (origination). Vertical rungs "01 Generate / 02 Personalize / 03 Deliver / 04 Land". Muted "Most AI stops here" tag on 01 (grayed); bright on-brand bracket grouping 02-04 labeled "The execution layer". Headline "AI can write the message. The win is what happens next." Cerkl brand colors, logo bottom corner, no headshot, text-only, 1080x1080, mobile-legible.

Asset: https://www.canva.com/design/DAHOJY7YUJU/edit
Manual finish: AI-origination SHELL ONLY (NEEDS DESIGNER). Resize to 1080x1080; build the numbered 4-rung stack (01 Generate grayed + "Most AI stops here" tag; 02 Personalize / 03 Deliver / 04 Land in Cobalt 60 with a bracket labeled "The execution layer"); headline verbatim "AI can write the message. The win is what happens next."; add Cerkl Broadcast tag + logo. Full spec: channels/linkedin/canva-manifests/static-theme_ai-execution-layers.yml

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Link post: AI in Microsoft Teams (wraps AI-in-Teams blog)
Description:
Topic: Link post — amplify the AI in Microsoft Teams blog (same-day, 7/8). Drive blog reads; mobile-EX pillar.
Post type: static-blog
Wraps: ai-in-microsoft-teams-where-native-ends
Copy:
Copilot can draft your announcement, summarize the thread, and post it to the channel in seconds. It cannot tell you who read it.

For internal comms, that is the gap that matters. Teams confirms the send and stays silent on the read. A channel post proves a message exists somewhere people could see it. It does not prove the 30 people who needed to act on a benefits deadline are among the 8 who left a thumbs-up, and the frontline crew who rarely open the app never had a chance to see it.

Native Teams is a strong collaboration tool. Comms delivery and measurement is a separate job: targeted delivery to the right audience, across more than one channel, with per-employee read tracking you can report on.

The new post draws the line between where native Teams AI is enough and where you have outgrown it, plus the four signals that tell you which side you are on: [Furqan inserts live cerkl.com/blog/ai-in-microsoft-teams-where-native-ends on publish]
